test_frontend.details: 207 passed, 5 failed (all 5 in BenchmarkPage.test.tsx due to getByText selector ambiguity — matching multiple DOM elements)

typecheck_backend.details: 99 errors in 15 files — pre-existing type errors scheduled for fix in milestone 'deployment' (feature deploy-mypy-fixes). Not introduced by benchmark-mode.

lint_frontend.details: 7 errors: (1) BenchmarkPage.tsx:2 unused 'useNavigate' import, (2) BenchmarkPage.tsx:159 unused 'currentBenchmarkId' variable, (3-4) HistoryPage.test.tsx:428,534 'HTMLInputElement' not defined, (5-6) SubmitPage.test.tsx:498,535 'HTMLInputElement' not defined, (7) SubmitPage.tsx:53 unused 'getScenarioDisplayLabel'. 12 warnings (complexity/max-lines — pre-existing).

validatorNote: Backend tests pass cleanly (303/303). Backend mypy has 99 pre-existing errors scheduled for deployment milestone — NOT introduced by benchmark-mode. Frontend TypeScript strict check passes. Frontend has 5 test failures (all in BenchmarkPage.test.tsx from selector ambiguity) and 7 ESLint errors (2 unused vars in BenchmarkPage.tsx, 4 HTMLInputElement undef in test files, 1 unused function in SubmitPage.tsx). These are all straightforward fixes — the BenchmarkPage functionality itself works correctly.

blockingIssues.description: ESLint error: 'useNavigate' imported from react-router-dom but never used in BenchmarkPage.tsx line 2. Remove the import.
ESLint error: 'currentBenchmarkId' assigned but never used in BenchmarkPage.tsx line 159. Remove the dead variable.

5 of 18 BenchmarkPage tests fail due to getByText selector ambiguity. Tests use getByText(/60/i), getByText(/failed/i), getByText(/high/i), getByText(/high.com/i), getByText('3') which match multiple DOM elements. Fix by using within() scoping with data-testid or getAllBy* variants.

ESLint 'no-undef' errors: 'HTMLInputElement' not defined in HistoryPage.test.tsx (lines 428, 534) and SubmitPage.test.tsx (lines 498, 535). Fix by adding /* global HTMLInputElement */ comments or configuring ESLint env for test files.
ESLint error: 'getScenarioDisplayLabel' function defined but never used in SubmitPage.tsx line 53. Remove the dead function.

nonBlockingIssues.description: BenchmarkListResponse schema defined but never used (dead code). benchmark_orchestrator.py: both branches of audit completion always set status='completed' — 'failed' only reachable via exception handler.

Migration file uses autogenerated hash naming (6e81669bfdad_) instead of YYYYMMDD_NNNN_ convention. Inline imports in benchmark_orchestrator.py. f-string in logger.critical() instead of structured logging.
key={index} on dynamic URL input list, overly permissive URL_REGEX (/^https?:\/\/.+/), double validation of minimum URLs, commit dd21bf7 includes 11+ unrelated files (backend changes, webm binaries).
Minor logic bug: `!score` treats score=0 as failed. Should use `score === null`. URL truncation threshold inconsistency (checks full URL length but truncates protocol-stripped version).

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md: changed '3 tables' to '4 tables: audits, findings, audit_events, benchmarks' to reflect new Benchmark model.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Workers must use selective git staging (e.g., git add frontend/ or git add specific-files) rather than git add -A, to prevent committing unrelated changes from the working tree. This is especially important for frontend features that should not include backend file changes.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: benchmark-frontend-submit commit dd21bf7 included 11+ unrelated files (backend/app/providers/browser.py, generate_webm.py, test .webm binaries) because the worker used 'git add -A'. This creates impure commit history and makes it harder to trace feature boundaries.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce that manual browser verification (Step 6) is MANDATORY and must be recorded in handoff.interactiveChecks. 3 of 4 benchmark-mode frontend workers reported empty interactiveChecks despite the skill stating this step is mandatory. If server startup fails during verification, the worker should document the failure as a deviation, not silently skip the step and claim procedure was followed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: benchmark-frontend-submit, benchmark-frontend-page, and benchmark-history-integration all have empty interactiveChecks arrays. benchmark-frontend-page and benchmark-history-integration handoffs claimed 'ESLint clean' which was false. This is a systemic pattern across multiple workers/features.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'ESLint test environment configuration: Test files (.test.ts, .test.tsx) may reference DOM globals like HTMLInputElement. Either configure ESLint with browser env for test file patterns, or use eslint-disable comments with explanation.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: HistoryPage.test.tsx and SubmitPage.test.tsx both use 'as HTMLInputElement' type assertions that fail ESLint no-undef. This will recur in future test files using DOM type assertions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'When refactoring code to extract shared utilities, run ESLint after refactoring to catch unused imports and dead functions at the original call site. Workers must remove unused imports and functions before committing.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: SubmitPage.tsx has unused getScenarioDisplayLabel function (introduced by benchmark-frontend-submit, not cleaned up by benchmark-history-integration). BenchmarkPage.tsx has unused useNavigate import and currentBenchmarkId variable. Multiple workers failed to clean up dead code after refactoring.

rejectedObservations.observation: Alembic migration naming convention mismatch (hash vs YYYYMMDD)
rejectedObservations.reason: The autogenerate command produces hash names by default. Changing this requires modifying alembic.ini/env.py which is outside the scope of individual features. The existing migrations already use both patterns. Not actionable without a dedicated cleanup task.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker committed BenchmarkPage inside benchmark-history-integration commit with placeholder commitId
rejectedObservations.reason: Git history metadata error. The code is in the repo and working correctly. The commitId discrepancy is a process issue, not a code issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: Test mock factories are duplicated across test files rather than shared
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a pre-existing pattern across the codebase (ReportPage.test.tsx, SubmitPage.test.tsx, etc. all define local factories). Changing this would require a broad refactoring effort across all test files, which is outside the scope of benchmark-mode features.

rejectedObservations.observation: TDD workflow from frontend-dev skill not followed (implementation before tests)
rejectedObservations.reason: While the skill prescribes TDD, the result is correct code with comprehensive tests. The ordering of writing tests vs code is a process concern that doesn't affect code quality. The existing tests cover the expected behaviors regardless of authoring order.
